channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Russian strikes kill three, wound four in Ukraine
item.description: Russian drone attacks in Ukraine's Sumy region killed two civilians and wounded four. The attacks occurred in the northeastern Ukraine border region, according to the head of Sumy’s regional military administration.

item.title: Loud bangs and a Trump evacuation: chaos at correspondents' dinner
item.description: A chaotic scene unfolded at the correspondents' dinner as the Secret Service swarmed the banquet hall and guests ducked under tables. The incident prompted an evacuation, reportedly involving Donald Trump.

item.title: Donald Trump says shooting won’t ‘deter’ him from Iran war
item.description: US President Donald Trump stated that a recent shooting incident wouldn't deter him from pursuing a potential war with Iran. He had previously canceled his envoys' trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran.

item.title: Donald Trump, JD Vance Unhurt After Shots Fired During White House Event: What We Know So Far
item.description: Shots were fired during a White House event attended by Donald Trump and JD Vance, but both were unharmed. The incident occurred at a press dinner. Trump, JD Vance Unhurt After Shots Fired During Press Dinner At White House.

item.title: Trump Event Shooting Live Updates: Trump Says Press Dinner Shooter Was A "Lone Wolf"
item.description: Donald Trump was evacuated after gunshots were heard at his event. Trump later described the press dinner shooter as a "lone wolf". Trump Event Shooting Live Updates: Trump Says Press Dinner Shooter Was A "Lone Wolf".

item.title: Burrata Salad, Then Chaos: Journalists Duck As Shots Fired At US Press Dinner
item.description: US journalists sought cover at a press dinner after shots were fired, with some witnesses describing the scene as chaotic. Journalists watched in shock and then sought cover under tables as shots were fired at the press dinner.

item.title: 4 vying to be the next UN chief try to set themselves apart as race heats up
item.description: Four candidates vying to become the next UN chief are trying to differentiate themselves as the selection process intensifies. 4 vying to be the next UN chief try to set themselves apart as race heats up WGN Radio 720.

item.title: China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi tells Myanmar leader Beijing will back its security, sovereignty
item.description: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi pledged support for Myanmar's security and sovereignty, saying Beijing will back the country's development path. Beijing supports Myanmar “in forging a successful development path that aligns with its national conditions and enjoys the support of its people”, Wang said, according to a Chinese ministry readout on Apr 25.

item.title: Oil, Geopolitics, and Cameco: Here's Where the Stock Could Be in 12 Months
item.description: Cameco's stock performance is expected to be influenced by oil prices and geopolitical factors in the next 12 months. Oil, Geopolitics, and Cameco: Here's Where the Stock Could Be in 12 Months The Motley Fool.

item.title: Panic and chaos as gunshots disrupt White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Gunshots disrupted the White House Correspondents' Dinner, prompting guests to take cover and follow instructions to stay down. Moments after what sounded like gunshots, cries of "Stay down!" and "Get down!" were heard while guests took cover.

item.title: Colombia blames dissident FARC rebel for deadly bus bomb
item.description: A bomb-laden bus exploded on Colombia's Pan-American Highway to Venezuela, killing at least 14 people. Colombia blames dissident FARC rebel for the deadly attack. A bomb-laden bus has exploded, killing at least 14 people on Colombia’s Pan-American Highway to Venezuela.

item.title: Trump criticizes NATO’s role in Iran conflict, questions US alliance commitment
item.description: US President Trump criticized NATO's role in the Iran conflict and questioned US commitment to the alliance. He made the comments in a recent public appearance. Trump criticizes NATO’s role in Iran conflict, questions US alliance commitment Crypto Briefing.

item.title: Karoline Leavitt's Uncanny "Shots Will Be Fired" Comment Before Trump Event
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt made a comment about "shots will be fired" before a Trump event. The context and details of the comment are not specified. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt (file).

item.title: Trump After Shooting During White House Press Dinner
item.description: A shooting threat occurred during the White House Press Dinner, attended by Trump. Trump praised law enforcement and reported that the shooter had been apprehended. Trump also praised law enforcement and said the shooter had been apprehended after a shooting threat.

item.title: What we know so far about suspected gunman at dinner attended by Trump
item.description: A suspect armed with multiple weapons and knives stormed a security checkpoint at the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington DC, shooting a Secret Service agent. Police provided details of the incident.

item.title: Trump shares picture of suspect under arrest: ‘Secret Service officers acted quickly and bravely’
item.description: US President Donald Trump shared a picture of a suspect under arrest, praising Secret Service officers for acting quickly and bravely. The officers apprehended the suspect. No further details were provided.

item.title: White House Press Dinner Shooter "Very Sick", Was "Heavily Armed": Trump
item.description: Trump described the White House Press Dinner shooter as "very sick" and mentioned the individual was "heavily armed". The shooter was involved in an incident at the dinner. US President Donald Trump addresses the press following the attack.

item.title: Gunshots, panic, and Trump’s evacuation: What happened at White House correspondents’ dinner
item.description: Gunshots caused panic at the White House correspondents' dinner, prompting Trump's evacuation. The incident unfolded with chaos. Trump and his wife were rushed to safety. Gunshots, panic, and Trump’s evacuation: What happened at White House correspondents’ dinner.

item.title: ‘Get down, get down!’ Trump and Melania rushed to safety as shots fired at White House dinner
item.description: Trump and First Lady Melania were rushed to safety with orders to "get down" as shots fired at the White House dinner. The couple's evacuation was part of the response.

item.title: Trump says 'lone wolf' shooter at correspondents' dinner had multiple weapons
item.description: Shots were heard at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, prompting guests to scramble for cover. Former US President Trump was unharmed in the incident. Guests at the black-tie White House Correspondents' Dinner scrambled to hide under tables.
